summary: Codex runtime direction supersedes further polishing of the failed shared Haystack backend; official CLI JSONL, immutable governed tools and execpolicy proved on a real complex HVAC turn.

Independent review rejected workspace-write, shared evidence, raw thread IDs, upload mismatch and substring-grounding. The adapter now uses fresh ephemeral sessions, elevated Windows Permission Profiles, a required official MCP server with three fixed tools, private DuckDB outside command access, structured exact-excerpt citations, sanitized failures and truthful fixed-synthetic UI. Fourteen runtime tests, fifty focused regressions, browser acceptance and Ruff pass; secure real-model acceptance remains blocked pending administrator approval for elevated sandbox setup.

The remaining independent-review findings were remediated with TDD: telemetry citations now require non-empty data_quality or cop_ranking results, Codex mode skips the legacy embedding/reranking stack, and run-codex performs an elevated allow/deny sandbox preflight before starting the Web service. Nineteen Codex-runtime tests and fifty-five focused runtime/direction/Web regressions pass; real-model acceptance remains blocked pending administrator approval for elevated sandbox setup.

Independent rereview found zero Critical and zero Important code findings after the secure startup, grounding and embedding-stack fixes. The branch remains blocked from merge or production claims because real elevated-sandbox and secure real-model acceptance require explicit administrator approval.

The Chairman authorized elevated Windows setup and UAC provisioning completed, but CodexSandboxOffline could still open application/root-repository files on the readable E: drive. The adapter now applies an explicit deny ACL to private DuckDB directories and requires an outside-source denial before writing a readiness marker, so Windows startup remains blocked. A no-model WSL2 Landlock probe with official Codex 0.144.5 passed workspace/private/root isolation and is the next backend candidate.

Post-UAC rereview found that schema-v1 two-probe markers could be reused after the application-source probe was added. The marker schema is now version 2, all older markers are rejected, and readiness requires workspace allow plus private-DuckDB and application-source denials. Earlier pre-UAC review conclusions are marked superseded.

The primary transport now uses the official beta openai-codex Python SDK 0.144.4 and its pinned bundled App Server with no silent CLI fallback. The official MCP server exposes eight bounded read-only HVAC tools, output supports governed tables/charts/citations, and the existing Cytoscape renderer provides an Obsidian-style compact evidence graph with full-project expand. Thirty-three runtime tests, sixty-nine focused tests, nine browser tests and Ruff pass; one real-model browser case remains deliberately gated because native Windows host-read isolation still fails.

Independent review reported no Critical and six Important findings. TDD remediation now binds virtual/data citations and numeric presentations to exact MCP evidence, rejects Shell/file-change/Web-search events, exposes search_project_knowledge, forces SDK App Server --strict-config, redacts internal paths server-side, aligns citation and graph locations, and publishes constrained nine-tool MCP schemas. Thirty-eight runtime tests, seventy-four runtime/direction/Web tests, nine evaluator-contract tests and nine browser tests pass; one browser real-model case remains intentionally gated and descendant timeout cleanup remains a documented minor risk.

Successive focused rereviews closed document-tool provenance, question-number and double-negative grounding, arbitrary citation-metadata path leakage, and exact virtual-dataset graph highlighting. The final independent reviewer reported no Critical or Important findings and Ready: Yes. Forty-four runtime tests, ninety combined focused tests and ten browser tests pass; one real-model browser case remains intentionally gated by the native-Windows host-read isolation boundary.

Commit cfa8dc8 delivered the official Codex Python SDK/App Server transport, nine governed MCP tools, structured presentations, exact evidence binding, Obsidian-style compact/full graph, tests, research and company deployment handoff to origin/codex/agentic-rag-bakeoff without force. The bounded ledger is complete; native-Windows company-data real-model execution remains a separate fail-closed WSL2/Landlock production-isolation goal.

Official OpenCode SDK/provider/MCP/permission docs and GitHub API evidence were refreshed. Existing simple and first-complex private payloads replayed grounded before new model calls; private payload bytes remain local and unpublished.

OpenCode 1.18.3 v1 SDK path/query/body mismatch reproduced as /session/%7Bid%7D and a hard timeout. TDD changed the worker to the generated path.id/query.directory/body contract; SDK source-shape and Node syntax checks pass.

TDD fixed governed wide-table grounding loss, bounded every event table to 12 columns, retained internal grounding_tables for non-display evidence, capped aggregate output at four tables/charts, and removed the duplicate pytz dependency.

Two predeclared same-model xhigh OpenCode cases completed on the synthetic corpus: CC01 194.939 seconds with three tools and six citations; CC02 151.046 seconds with three tools and six citations, grounded after local replay and safe write/control refusal. A live Codex control remains fail-closed behind native-Windows isolation.

Closed fixed-Agent legacy egress, discarded-citation numeric grounding and Windows process-tree cleanup findings with TDD. Taskkill nonzero, timeout and OSError now fail closed; independent rereview returned Ready: Yes.
Recorded machine-readable Codex blocked_by_isolation artifacts and a separate CC02 semantic contract correction without rewriting the frozen comparison contract.
Final release gate passed after all fixes: Ruff check/format passed, 448 tests passed, 14 intentional tests skipped, and release guard passed.
